entities.name: WATER HYACINTH
entities.description: A floating invasive aquatic weed (Pontederia crassipes) with high proliferation and adaptability, serving as a source of lignocellulosic biomass.

entities.name: BIOENERGY
entities.description: Sustainable energy generated from biomass, focusing on reducing reliance on non-renewable resources.

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: Organic material used as a primary resource for the bioeconomy and energy production.

entities.name: BIOECONOMY
entities.description: An economic system based on the sustainable use of biological resources to foster biodiversity and sustainable development.

entities.name: BIODIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of biological life, which is a critical component for a viable bioeconomy supply-demand cycle.

entities.name: PROLIFERATION RATE
entities.description: The speed at which an organism increases in number, contributing to the invasive nature of water hyacinth.

entities.name: ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of an organism to adjust to different environmental conditions.

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS
entities.description: The external factors that influence the growth and proliferation of aquatic weeds.

entities.name: LIGNOCELLULOSIC BIOMASS
entities.description: Plant-derived biomass composed of cellulose, hemicellulose, and lignin, used as feed for biofuels.

entities.name: BIOFUEL
entities.description: Fuel derived from biomass, representing a sustainable energy alternative.

entities.name: CHEMICAL METHODS
entities.description: Techniques using chemical agents to control the growth of invasive aquatic weeds.

entities.name: AMMONIA
entities.description: A foul-smelling substance released during the decomposition of rotting biomass.

entities.name: BIOCHEMICAL CONVERSION
entities.description: A process of converting biomass into useful products using biological agents.

entities.name: THERMOCHEMICAL CONVERSION
entities.description: A process of converting biomass into useful products using heat.

entities.name: PHYSIO-CHEMICAL CONVERSION
entities.description: A process of converting biomass into useful products using physical and chemical means.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS
entities.description: Global objectives aimed at fostering sustainable development and a green bioeconomy.
